theorem_title: Weak solution equals unique minimizer of the Dirichlet energy
theorem_statement:
Weak solution equals unique minimizer of the Dirichlet energy.

Let \(\Omega \subset \mathbb{R}^d\) be a bounded domain with sufficiently regular boundary, and let \(f \in L^2(\Omega)\). Define
\[
J(v) := \frac12 \int_\Omega |\nabla v|^2 \, dx - \int_\Omega f v \, dx
\qquad \text{for } v \in H_0^1(\Omega).
\]

Then \(u \in H_0^1(\Omega)\) satisfies
\[
\int_\Omega \nabla u \cdot \nabla \varphi \, dx
=
\int_\Omega f \varphi \, dx
\qquad \text{for every } \varphi \in H_0^1(\Omega)
\]
if and only if \(u\) is the unique minimizer of \(J\) over \(H_0^1(\Omega)\).

nodes.title: Main equivalence: weak solution \(\Leftrightarrow\) unique minimizer of \(J\)
nodes.informal_statement:
For \(u\in H_0^1(\Omega)\), the weak formulation
\[
\int_\Omega \nabla u\cdot \nabla \varphi\,dx=\int_\Omega f\varphi\,dx\quad \forall\,\varphi\in H_0^1(\Omega)
\]
is equivalent to \(u\) being the unique minimizer of
\[
J(v)=\frac12\int_\Omega |\nabla v|^2\,dx-\int_\Omega f v\,dx
\]
over \(H_0^1(\Omega)\).
nodes.informal_proof_text: Combine the two directions: a weak solution satisfies the exact energy gap identity, hence minimizes uniquely; conversely, a minimizer has vanishing first variation along every direction, which is exactly the weak equation.

nodes.title: Energy gap identity from the weak equation
nodes.informal_statement:
If \(u\in H_0^1(\Omega)\) satisfies the weak equation, then for every \(v\in H_0^1(\Omega)\) one has
\[
J(v)=J(u)+\frac12\int_\Omega |\nabla(v-u)|^2\,dx.
\]
In particular, \(J(v)\ge J(u)\), with equality only when \(v=u\) in \(H_0^1(\Omega)\).
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Write \(v=u+w\) with \(w\in H_0^1(\Omega)\), expand \(|
abla(u+w)|^2\), and regroup terms as \(J(u)\) plus a linear bracket plus \( rac12\int |
abla w|^2\). The weak formulation with test function \(\varphi=w\) kills the linear bracket, leaving the exact gap identity. Nonnegativity of the last term gives minimality, and vanishing of the gap forces \(
abla(v-u)=0\), hence \(v=u\) in \(H_0^1(\Omega)\).
nodes.formalization_rationale: Best formal island: the exact energy decomposition is compact, reusable, and directly yields both minimality and uniqueness from the weak equation.

formal_artifact.lean_statement: theorem energy_gap_identity_of_weak_solution {E : Type*} [SeminormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] {u f v : E} (hweak : ∀ w : E, inner ℝ u w = inner ℝ f w) : (1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖v‖ ^ 2 - inner ℝ f v = ((1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖u‖ ^ 2 - inner ℝ f u) + (1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖v - u‖ ^ 2
formal_artifact.lean_code:
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.Basic

namespace FormalIslands

/--
If a vector `u` satisfies the weak equation against every test vector, then the quadratic energy
splits into the value at `u` plus the exact nonnegative gap `1/2 ‖v - u‖^2`.
-/
theorem energy_gap_identity_of_weak_solution
    {E : Type*} [SeminormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E]
    {u f v : E}
    (hweak : ∀ w : E, inner ℝ u w = inner ℝ f w) :
    (1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖v‖ ^ 2 - inner ℝ f v =
      ((1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖u‖ ^ 2 - inner ℝ f u) + (1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖v - u‖ ^ 2 := by
  have hv : v = u + (v - u) := by
    symm
    exact add_sub_cancel u v
  have hweak' : inner ℝ u (v - u) = inner ℝ f (v - u) := hweak (v - u)
  calc
    (1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖v‖ ^ 2 - inner ℝ f v
        = (1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖u + (v - u)‖ ^ 2 - inner ℝ f (u + (v - u)) := by
          conv_lhs => rw [hv]
    _ = (1 / 2 : ℝ) * (‖u‖ ^ 2 + 2 * inner ℝ u (v - u) + ‖v - u‖ ^ 2) -
          (inner ℝ f u + inner ℝ f (v - u)) := by
        rw [← real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq (x := u + (v - u)),
          real_inner_add_add_self, inner_add_right,
          real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq (x := u),
          real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq (x := v - u)]
    _ = ((1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖u‖ ^ 2 - inner ℝ f u) + (1 / 2 : ℝ) * ‖v - u‖ ^ 2 := by
        rw [hweak']
        ring

end FormalIslands

nodes.title: First variation of \(J\) along lines
nodes.informal_statement:
If \(u\in H_0^1(\Omega)\) is a minimizer of \(J\), then for every \(\varphi\in H_0^1(\Omega)\) the function \(g(t)=J(u+t\varphi)\) has \(g'(0)=0\), and
\[
g'(0)=\int_\Omega \nabla u\cdot \nabla \varphi\,dx-\int_\Omega f\varphi\,dx.
\]
Therefore \(u\) satisfies the weak formulation.
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Fix \(\varphi\in H_0^1(\Omega)\) and consider the one-variable restriction \(g(t)=J(u+t\varphi)\). Since \(u\) minimizes \(J\), \(t=0\) is a minimum of \(g\), so \(g'(0)=0\). Expanding \(|
abla(u+t\varphi)|^2\) gives a quadratic polynomial in \(t\), whose derivative at \(0\) is exactly \(\int_\Omega 
abla u\cdot
abla\varphi\,dx-\int_\Omega f\varphi\,dx\). Setting this equal to zero yields the weak equation.
nodes.formalization_rationale: Second key formal island: the one-dimensional variation argument is the cleanest route from minimizer to weak formulation and is easy to formalize locally.

edges.label: weak solution => minimizer
edges.explanation: The energy gap identity yields existence of a minimizer and uniqueness by strict positivity of the gap unless \(v=u\).

edges.label: minimizer => weak solution
edges.explanation: Vanishing first variation along every direction recovers the Euler-Lagrange equation, which is the weak formulation.
